Dinosaur facts: The Tyrannosaurus rex had teeth up to 12 inches long.
Stegosaurus had a brain the size of a walnut.
Some dinosaurs had feathers, not just scales.
Triceratops had up to 800 teeth, constantly replaced.
Dinosaur movie quotes (e.g., Jurassic Pa: Your scientists were so preoccupied with whether or not they could, they didn’t stop to think if they should.
Art of War quotes: All warfare is based on deception.
If you know the enemy and know yourself, you need not fear the result of a hundred battles.
In the midst of chaos, there is also opportunity.
Victorious warriors win first and then go to war.
The supreme art of war is to subdue the enemy without fighting.
Zen proverbs: Before enlightenment; chop wood, carry water. After enlightenment; chop wood, carry water.
Sitting quietly, doing nothing, spring comes, and the grass grows by itself.
When the student is ready, the teacher will appear.
Bushido code excerpts: Rectitude is the power of deciding upon a certain course of conduct in accordance with reason.
Courage is doing what is right.
Benevolence brings peace to the world.
Politeness is a poor virtue if it is motivated by fear.
Honor is the consciousness of personal dignity.
Sci-fi classic book quotes: I must not fear. Fear is the mind-killer. (Dune)
The stars are not for man. (Foundation)
Don’t Panic. (Hitchhiker’s Guide to the Galaxy)
Reality is that which, when you stop believing in it, doesn’t go away. (Philip K. Dick)
Sci-fi movie quotes (pre-2000): I’ve seen things you people wouldn’t believe. (Blade Runner)
The needs of the many outweigh the needs of the few. (Star Trek II)
May the Force be with you. (Star Wars)
Bukowski quotes: Find what you love and let it kill you.
What matters most is how well you walk through the fire.
We are here to laugh at the odds and live our lives so well that Death will tremble to take us.
Some people never go crazy. What truly horrible lives they must lead.
An intellectual says a simple thing in a hard way. An artist says a hard thing in a simple way.
Stoic quotes (Seneca, Marcus Aurelius, E: We suffer more often in imagination than in reality. (Seneca)
The impediment to action advances action. What stands in the way becomes the way. (Marcus Aurelius)
It is not things themselves that disturb us, but our opinions about them. (Epictetus)
He who fears death will never do anything worth of a man who is alive. (Seneca)
You have power over your mind—not outside events. Realize this, and you will find strength. (Marcus Aurelius)
Punk rock lyrics (classic era): London calling to the faraway towns. (The Clash)
I am an antichrist, I am an anarchist. (Sex Pistols)
